stories.title: World Cup Reacts: Which big team’s in trouble, who will play Cinderella?
Marino, Parrino, Turner
stories.summary:
My apologies in advance to anyone I may have left out.



	 



	I'm an older guy, and I'm not very plugged into the internet in all of its modern forms. For example I'm not a podcast guy. However, recently I've watched a Parrino podcast and a Marino podcast, and I regularly look at stuff on Cover one, and I've developed a new appreciation for the quality of a lot of the coverage about the Bills that is out there. I know I left out some of the print media people who also are good, but I tend to see their work more often.



	 



	I just wanted to say that we're fortunate to have these people who do quality interviews with Bills coaches and players, and who do thoughtful analysis about what's going on with the team.

stories.title: Buchanan, Smith named finalists for NLL Awards
stories.summary: Buffalo Bandits forwards Dhane Smith and Kyle Buchanan have been named finalists for the NLL’s end-of-season awards, the league announced Wednesday.   Smith was tabbed as a finalist for both NLL MVP and Offensive Player of the Year while Buchanan is up for the NLL’s Sportsmanship Award.   It’s the second consecutive season both players are finalists for their respective awards as Smith is the reigning Offensive […]

stories.title: Favorite/most exciting adequate at best quarterback in Bills history
stories.summary:
Keeping the theme of not the best quarterbacks in Bills history, who was the most fun to watch or your favorite almost capable quarterback (I am leaving off Fitz - as he may fall into this category, but I think he is just good enough to qualify as adequate, plus he is just too awesome).  



	 



	My vote is AVP - total doughboy who could just rip bombs.

stories.title: Would you take less salary to win a Super Bowl?
stories.summary:
This came up in the Knicks thread, I think it's a good question:



	 



	Why don't more QBs take a serious pay cut to have a better chance to win a Super Bowl? I would happily earn 40/million a year instead of 60/million if it upped my chances of getting a ring by 35-40%. Lifelong happiness of accomplishing the ultimate. 



	 



	This is probably why I'm not a billionaire (hahaha), but if you have 10 million dollars in the bank, I don't feel like money should be your prime motivating factor. Ultra-wealthy people and their chlidren are not happier than people who make enough to live comfortably, from what I see.

stories.title: CFL changes bring it closer to the NFL game
stories.summary:
100 yard field, smaller end zones, goal posts at the back line, not goal line, redefining the 1-point rouge, 35 second play clock... Not everyone is happy. 



	 



	CFL makes multiple NFL-style changes to the Canadian game | Daily Hive | Sports https://share.google/2aFkGOOhi03Eo7qEI



	 



	BC Lions QB Rourke sounds off on CFL's 'garbage' NFL-style changes | Daily Hive | Sports https://share.google/gxwv0Lc2b5QpySG1Q



	 



	Diehard CFL fans blast the league's NFL-style changes | Daily Hive | Sports https://share.google/gklogQ5ZXZKDc59Sb

stories.title: 2026 UFL thread.
stories.summary:
1. 60+ yard field goals = 4 points
	2.  One foot in bounds = catch
	3. Red zone penalties revert to half the distance to the goal. 
	4. The "Tush Push" is eliminated.
	5. No punting inside the opponent’s 50-yard line.
	6. Overtime: 3 alternating attempts per team from the 5 yard line.
	 
	7. Point aftertouchdown options:
	- 1 point – Kick from the 33-yard line
	- 2 points – Ball on the 2-yard line
	- 3 points – Ball on the 8-yard line



	 



	Season starts Friday, March 27.

stories.title: Time to Retire Mr Briteside
stories.summary:
Awful karma - it’s stolen valor - not our song.



	 



	Moment it was played at the two minute warning was the moment the nail drove through the coffin in this game



	 



	Seeing Mack Hollins pump his offense up while it played was nauseating

stories.title: Former 1st round #12 pick Henry Ruggs: 3 to 10 yrs prison for drunk driving fatal crash
stories.summary:
https://www.espn.com/nfl/story/_/id/38162272/ex-raiders-wr-henry-ruggs-iii-sentenced-3-10-years-fatal-dui



	 



	
		Quote
	

	
		
			Ruggs, 24, had been under house arrest with alcohol and location electronic monitoring devices since pleading guilty in May to one count of DUI resulting in death and one count of misdemeanor vehicular manslaughter in the crash that killed Tina Tintor and her dog, Max. On Wednesday, Ruggs read from a statement directed at Tintor's family in which he apologized for his actions.
		

		
			"To the parents and family of Ms. Tintor, I sincerely apologize for the pain and suffering," Ruggs said.
		
	



	 



	Pretty sure Ms Tintor's family don't think 3 years is nearly enough punishment for burning her alive.
	
	Ruggs got what his attorneys asked for:



	
		Quote
	

	
		
			In court documents filed before the sentencing hearing, Ruggs' attorneys had asked the judge to keep the duration of their client's prison time to a range of three to 10 years. Chesnoff and Richard Schonfeld said Ruggs "wishes that he could turn back time and change the outcome of the tragic events of November 2, 2021."
		
	



	 



	I'm sure he does, what with being cut from the team and losing his career and all.  But he can't.
